message:
feat(abi): port mseal + memfd_secret + rseq + cachestat (modern Linux syscall wrappers) (bd-4ny4d)

Four more modern Linux libc syscall wrappers, each forwarded via
libc::syscall with errno bridging through set_abi_errno:

  - mseal(addr, len, flags) -> int (Linux 6.10+, glibc 2.40,
    SYS_mseal=462): seal a memory region against future
    mprotect/munmap/mremap.
  - memfd_secret(flags) -> int (Linux 5.14+, SYS_memfd_secret=447):
    create an anonymous file backed by memory inaccessible from
    /proc/PID/mem and ptrace.
  - rseq(*rseq, len, flags, sig) -> int (Linux 4.18+, SYS_rseq=334):
    register or unregister a per-thread `struct rseq` for restartable
    sequences. NULL pointer guard returns EFAULT.
  - cachestat(fd, *cstat_range, *cstat, flags) -> int (Linux 6.5+,
    syscall 451 — embedded as a literal because libc 0.2.185 doesn't
    expose SYS_cachestat yet): query page cache statistics for a file
    range. NULL pointer guards return EFAULT.

A small `raw_syscall_with_errno` helper centralises the errno bridge
for these direct libc::syscall returns.

Tests: 5 abi tests covering mseal happy path (with verified post-seal
munmap rejection) and supported errno fallbacks; memfd_secret
(accepts ENOSYS / EOPNOTSUPP / EPERM as kernel/sandbox fallbacks);
rseq EFAULT on NULL; cachestat EFAULT on NULL pointers and either
data-or-ENOSYS on a real 4 KiB temp file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-24x","title":"Math relevance ledger: map every runtime_math module to a concrete runtime decision","description":"Critique mapping: #5.

Deliverables:
- For each module: decision target, invariant, evidence inputs, action outputs, and fallback when data missing.
- Modules without concrete decision linkage are marked ResearchOnly.

Acceptance:
- Ledger is complete and machine-readable.
- No module remains in default production path without decision linkage.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"Delivered runtime_math linkage ledger + CI gate.  {"id":"bd-25n","title":"Unit test expansion program: exhaustive positive/negative/property tests by subsystem","description":"Goal: require deep unit test coverage, not shallow happy-path checks.

- Deterministic seeds and reproducible fixtures for all nontrivial logic.
- Property/invariant tests for high-risk components (allocator, threading, runtime math).

Acceptance:
- Unit coverage matrix reaches declared target for all critical symbols/subsystems.
- Failure output includes symbol name, mode, and invariant details.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"Fourth increment complete (2026-02-11): expanded barrier admissibility tests in crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/barrier.rs. Added tests: request_size_limit_is_inclusive; strict_pointer_bypass_does_not_override_request_size_guard; strict_pointer_bypass_turns_off_at_high_risk_and_rejects_fast_write.  {"id":"bd-25pf","title":"bd-rqn subtask: Production-set change policy gate (admission/retirement evidence)","description":"Background:
- Future additions/removals must preserve governance integrity.

Goal:
- Implement policy gate for production kernel changes (admission, retirement, and documentation consistency).

Deliverables:
1) Gate requiring classification + linkage + value proof artifacts for any production-set change.
2) Policy documentation and enforcement tests.
3) Failure diagnostics guiding contributors to missing evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Production set cannot change without complete evidence package.
- Gate outputs actionable diagnostics for missing items.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for policy gate logic.
- Integration gate tests with intentional failure fixtures.
- Structured logs for policy decision traces and missing-evidence reasons.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyWaterfall","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.779871133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:00:01.927214573Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T02:00:01.927192602Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","governance","math","verification"],"comments":[{"id":727,"issue_id":"bd-25pf","author":"SnowyWaterfall","text":"Claimed by SnowyWaterfall. Starting implementation of production-set change policy gate: enforce classification + linkage + value-proof evidence for production manifest deltas, with actionable diagnostics and tests.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:53:18Z"},{"id":728,"issue_id":"bd-25pf","author":"SnowyWaterfall","text":"Completed implementation for bd-25pf.\n\nDelivered:\n- Added policy artifact: tests/conformance/math_production_set_policy.v1.json\n- Added gate script: scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh\n- Wired into extended CI: scripts/ci.sh\n- Added harness integration test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/math_production_set_policy_test.rs\n\nGate behavior:\n- Enforces production-set digest lock (manifest SHA + count).\n- Requires classification + linkage evidence for every production-manifest module.\n- Requires value-proof evidence for production_core/production_monitor modules.\n- Requires waiver + migration-wave evidence for research modules still in production manifest.\n- Emits actionable diagnostics + structured JSONL logs + JSON report.\n\nVerification run:\n- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh  (PASS)\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_production_set_policy_test -- --nocapture  (4 passed)","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:00:01Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-25xxp","title":"PORT: openat2 + futex_waitv (modern Linux libc syscall wrappers)","description":"POSIX/Linux libc wrappers for two modern syscalls. (1) openat2(dirfd, pathname, *open_how, size) -> int (Linux 5.6, glibc 2.34) — extended openat that takes a versioned struct open_how { u64 flags; u64 mode; u64 resolve; } describing flags + RESOLVE_* policy bits. Just dispatch SYS_openat2 (= 437 on x86_64) via libc::syscall. (2) futex_waitv(*waiters, nr_futexes, flags, *timeout, clockid) -> int (Linux 5.16, glibc 2.35). Wait on multiple futexes simultaneously; returns the index of the woken futex on success, -1 + errno on timeout/error. Forward to the existing frankenlibc_core::syscall::sys_futex_waitv. NULL-pointer / negative-count guards plus errno bridging.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:47:51.138356601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:47:51.138356601Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

(1) openat2(dirfd, pathname, *open_how, size) -> int (Linux 5.6, glibc 2.34) — extended openat that takes a versioned struct open_how { u64 flags; u64 mode; u64 resolve; } describing flags + RESOLVE_* policy bits. Just dispatch SYS_openat2 (= 437 on x86_64) via libc::syscall. (2) futex_waitv(*waiters, nr_futexes, flags, *timeout, clockid) -> int (Linux 5.16, glibc 2.35). Wait on multiple futexes simultaneously; returns the index of the woken futex on success, -1 + errno on timeout/error. Forward to the existing frankenlibc_core::syscall::sys_futex_waitv. NULL-pointer / negative-count guards plus errno bridging.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:47:51.138356601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:51:40.511340271Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:51:40.509316267Z","close_reason":"shipped: openat2 (SYS_openat2 syscall + errno bridge) + futex_waitv (forwards to core sys_futex_waitv); 6 abi tests including happy-path open+read, struct-size validation, NULL guards, and ETIMEDOUT on past deadline","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2625","title":"bd-25n subtask: Strict-vs-hardened divergence bounds test suite","description":"Background:
- Strict vs hardened divergence must remain controlled and explainable.

Goal:
- Build dedicated test suite validating strict/hardened divergence bounds and policy consistency.

Deliverables:
1) Expected divergence matrix for representative APIs/families.
2) Tests asserting when divergence is allowed, required, or forbidden.
3) Alarm tests for unexpected divergence drift.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Divergence behavior is explicit and enforced.
- Unexpected divergence triggers deterministic failures with evidence.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit/integration tests in both modes.
- Structured logs including mode_pair, api_family, divergence_class, outcome.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverLake","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:39.300065159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:40:09.772696056Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T00:40:09.772672351Z","close_reason":"Implemented runtime_math strict-vs-hardened divergence bounds suite (matrix + harness gate + CI + tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","modes","testing","unit","verification"],"comments":[{"id":729,"issue_id":"bd-2625","author":"SilverLake","text":"Implemented strict-vs-hardened divergence bounds suite.

Evidence/spec:
- tests/runtime_math/runtime_math_divergence_bounds.v1.json (evaluation + required cases; forbidden divergence rules)

Harness gate + report:
- crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/runtime_math_divergence_bounds.rs
- cargo run -p frankenlibc-harness --bin harness -- runtime-math-divergence-bounds --workspace-root . --log target/conformance/runtime_math_divergence_bounds.log.jsonl --report target/conformance/runtime_math_divergence_bounds.report.json

CI integration:
- scripts/check_runtime_math_divergence_bounds.sh
- scripts/ci.sh (FRANKENLIBC_EXTENDED_GATES=1)

Tests:
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_divergence_bounds_test -- --nocapture

Artifacts:
- target/conformance/runtime_math_divergence_bounds.log.jsonl
- target/conformance/runtime_math_divergence_bounds.report.json

Verification matrix row updated to complete.","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:40:03Z"}]}

Added scripts/profile_pipeline.sh (CPU flamegraph + allocation perf + syscall strace profiles) with timestamped artifact storage under target/profiles/<run_ts>/<mode> and automatic top-5 extraction per benchmark/profile type. Updated scripts/PROFILING_RUNTIME_MATH.md and README.md with canonical usage. Validated full strict critical run: MODE=strict PROFILE_TIME=1 scripts/profile_pipeline.sh produced 12 top-5 files across 4 critical benchmarks (runtime_math_decide, runtime_math_observe_fast, runtime_math_decide_observe, membrane_validate_known). Artifact round: target/profiles/20260211T025848Z/strict.","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:00:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-26xb","title":"EPIC: Apollo Accretive Value Program (Idea-Wizard 15)","description":"## Background
Accretive idea program to increase practical value for Apollo/FrankenLibC users without duplicating existing open-bead functionality.

## Design
Use `idea-wizard` methodology: broad ideation, ruthless winnow, overlap checks against all open beads, and operationalization into dependency-linked tasks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 15 additive ideas are represented as executable beads with clear non-overlap rationale.
- Each idea has concrete implementation shape, objective value signal, and closure gates.
- Dependencies are wired to existing foundations so execution is coherent and low-risk.

## Notes
Flagship set emphasizes debug velocity, confidence, explainability, and compute-pressure resilience.

## Success Criteria
All 15 idea tasks are closed with objective evidence, no unresolved overlap conflicts, and release-ready proof of accretive user value.","design":"Top-5 flagship ideas: autoreducer pipeline, change-impact oracle scheduler, compatibility SLO/certification packs, explainability workbench, counterfactual policy simulator.
Next-best-10: determinism sentinel, ABI auto-bisect, evidence tiering, shadow differential runner, spec-hole detector, synthetic workload composer, scaffold generator, tail-latency decomposition, CI risk-budget autopilot, incremental proof cache.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Epic closure requires all 15 ideas to be implemented or explicitly retired with replacement rationale and recorded decision evidence.\n\n### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Tooling hard requirement applies: use `/dp/asupersync` for deterministic orchestration/traceability and `/dp/frankentui` for deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI analysis output.","notes":"Non-overlap audit source: all 201 open beads were scanned by title/label/search before creating these tasks.

This epic is complementary to `bd-w2c3` and does not replace existing parity-closure obligations.\n\n### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set documented in bead comments/notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

Tooling contract: closure requires evidence of `/dp/asupersync` orchestration/traceability integration and `/dp/frankentui` deterministic diff/snapshot analysis integration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:56:52.756733844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:36.609936052Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:36.609716937Z","close_reason":"Flagship deliverable: code + artifacts in working tree.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accretive","apollo-value","execution","feature-parity","gap-closure","idea-wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26xb","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-26xb","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":731,"issue_id":"bd-26xb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Created via idea-wizard workflow:
- Top-5 flagship ideas selected for immediate value multipliers.
- Next-best-10 ideas captured as additive roadmap tasks.
- Each task includes mandatory unit/e2e/logging/tooling closure gates.
- Dependencies tie ideas to existing parity-closure foundations to prevent overlap drift.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:58:14Z"},{"id":732,"issue_id":"bd-26xb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-4ia","title":"Stub priority ranking: dynamic call-frequency traces from LD_PRELOAD smoke runs","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Instrument smoke runs to collect actual unresolved/stubbed symbol hit counts.
- Produce ranked burn-down list (top-N by runtime frequency x severity).

Acceptance:
- Ranking is reproducible and feeds next-wave implementation beads automatically.
- Top list is included in nightly evidence report.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): stub_priority_test (7 pass) + gate script. Scores match formula, tiers consistent, burn-down consistent, ranked symbols match support matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.737502712Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"Stub priority ranking operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_stub_priority.sh validates ranking, tier assignments, burn-down, and formula consistency.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","stubs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4ia","depends_on_id":"bd-2cr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4l5om","title":"PORT: __wait3/4 + __waitid + __dup3 + __pipe2 + __getcwd + __getlogin + __getlogin_r + __fcntl64 + __clock_settime + __clock_nanosleep (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for already-shipped public functions: __wait3, __wait4, __waitid (process_abi); __dup3, __pipe2 (io_abi); __getcwd, __getlogin, __getlogin_r, __fcntl64 (unistd_abi); __clock_settime, __clock_nanosleep (time_abi). glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols libstdc++ / NSS modules / libdl / libpthread internals link against. Same delegating-alias pattern as the previously-shipped __pthread_*, __libc_dlopen_mode, __strchrnul / __memrchr / __getline alias families. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers that delegate to the public names. +{"id":"bd-4ny4d","title":"PORT: mseal + memfd_secret + rseq + cachestat (modern Linux syscall wrappers)","description":"Add four modern Linux syscall wrappers. (1) mseal(addr, len, flags) -> int (Linux 6.10+, glibc 2.40, SYS_mseal=462) — seal a memory region against future remap/protect/munmap. (2) memfd_secret(flags) -> int (Linux 5.14+, SYS_memfd_secret=447) — create an anonymous file backed by memory inaccessible from /proc, ptrace, etc. (3) rseq(*rseq, len, flags, sig) -> int (Linux 4.18+, SYS_rseq=334) — register a restartable sequences area for per-CPU concurrency. (4) cachestat(fd, *cstat_range, *cstat, flags) -> int (Linux 6.5+, syscall 451) — query page cache statistics for a file range. All four forward via libc::syscall with errno bridging via set_abi_errno; opaque pointers for the structs since their layouts are kernel-version-specific. NULL-pointer guards where applicable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:55:37.463706734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:55:37.463706734Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
